pages.raw_text:
In the superior court of the state
of Washington. in and for the county
of Spokane.


Spokane international Railway
company, a corporation, plaintiff, VS.
S. d. Paddock and Mrs. s. d. Pad
dock. his wife, defendants.


To S. ID. Paddock and to Mrs. s. d.
Paddock. his wife;


You and each of you will please
take notice that the Spokane Interna-
tional Railway company has filed In
the superior court of the state of
Washington for Spokane county a Pc
tition directed to the said court, set
ting forth that the Spokane Interna-
tioual Railway company is n domestic
corporation engaged in the construc-
ton and operation of a line of rail-
way from the city of Spokane, thence
easterly to n point near the town of
Rathirum in the county of Kootenai.
state of Idaho. aud thence in a north
oasterly direction up the valley of
the Mooyie river to the International
boundary line between the United
States and Canada; that it is essen
tial for the operation of its lines of
railway. and the accommodation of
the public using the same, that 1t
acquire those lands situate, in Spo-
kane county, Washington, and de-
scribed as lots one Cd and two ca
In block two (2), and lots fourteen
GD and fifteen GO in block four
ca, all in River Front addition to
Spokane Falls (now Spokane), state
of Washington, for use for its right
of way for the line of its said rail
way, and for necessary sidetracks,
depots, depot grounds and terminal
yards; that the defendant, S ID. Pad
dock, appears to be the sole owner In
fee of said realty. ana that Mrs. 8. ID.
Paddock, the wife Of said S. II. Pad
dock, claims to have some interest In
said premises; that said petition
prays that the court empanel a jury
to ascertain the compensation to be
made in money to the owner of said
realty, and to all others interested
therein, for the taking of said realty
by petitioner for the purpose afore-
said;


And you and each of you are hereby
notified that on the 81 day of July,
1905. petitioner will apply to the SUI
perior court of the state OF Washing
ton for Spokane county, ana to the
Honorable William II. Huneke, Judge
thereof, Mt his courtroom In the city
of Spokane. at the hour of 9:30 a. m,
or as soon thereafter as counsel for
petitioner can be heard, for hearing
upon said petition, and an adjudica-
ton by the court that the taking of
said realty Is for public use, and is
necessary to the business of petit
TONER. and for the fixing OF a time
and place at which jury may be
called to determine the compensation
to be made therefor.


Dated this Sth day of June, 1905.


ALLEN & ALLEN,


Attorneys for Petitioner.


p. o. address: 61S The Rookery,
Spokane, Wash.
